Following Instructions at School.name: Classroom How-To Guide
Following Instructions at School.overview: Students create a step-by-step written guide for a classroom task (e.g., organizing supplies, setting up for an activity), using imperatives and sequencing words like 'first, next, and then'.
Following Instructions at School.general_objective: Develop instruction-writing skills using sequencing language and imperatives.
specific_objectives: Use imperatives and sequencing words correctly
Describe a classroom task step by step
Following Instructions at School.technology_integration: Use digital tools to create and share the guide.

Following Instructions at School.name: Instructional Video
Following Instructions at School.overview: Students record a video demonstrating a classroom routine, such as cleaning up or preparing for a project, using imperatives and collaborative language like 'let's' and 'we'.
Following Instructions at School.general_objective: Develop oral communication and digital production skills.
specific_objectives: Use imperatives and collaborative language in video
Following Instructions at School.technology_integration: Use video recording and editing tools for instructional videos.

Following Instructions at School.name: Task Flowchart
Following Instructions at School.overview: Students design a flowchart outlining steps in completing a task (e.g., art project or science experiment) with sequencing words and imperatives.
Following Instructions at School.general_objective: Develop organizational and visual communication skills.
specific_objectives: Design a clear flowchart with steps
Apply critical thinking to task organization
Following Instructions at School.technology_integration: Use digital tools for flowchart creation.

Taking Care of Our Class.name: Classroom Clean-Up Plan
Taking Care of Our Class.overview: Students develop a written plan outlining roles and responsibilities for keeping the classroom clean, using imperatives and present continuous tense (e.g., 'We are sweeping the floors.').
Taking Care of Our Class.general_objective: Develop responsibility and collaborative planning skills.
specific_objectives: Use imperatives and present continuous correctly
Taking Care of Our Class.technology_integration: Use digital design tools for the plan.

Taking Care of Our Class.name: Cleaning Instruction Poster
Taking Care of Our Class.overview: Students design posters with illustrated instructions for cleaning tasks, using imperatives and collaborative language (e.g., 'Let's arrange the chairs.').
Taking Care of Our Class.general_objective: Develop visual communication and language skills.
specific_objectives: Design an organized and attractive poster
Taking Care of Our Class.technology_integration: Use digital design tools for posters.

Taking Care of Our Class.name: Classroom Responsibility Chart
Taking Care of Our Class.overview: Students create a responsibility chart on paper or digitally, assigning weekly tasks to classmates, using present continuous and imperatives.
Taking Care of Our Class.general_objective: Develop organizational and digital literacy skills.
specific_objectives: Assign weekly tasks using a chart
Taking Care of Our Class.technology_integration: Use digital tools for chart creation.

The Local Farm Stand.name: Farm Stand Role-Play
The Local Farm Stand.overview: Students participate in a role-play simulating a farm stand transaction, using polite requests and WH-questions (e.g., 'How much is the pineapple?' 'I need two papayas, please.').
The Local Farm Stand.general_objective: Develop transactional communication and role-play skills.
specific_objectives: Practice vocabulary for fruits and vegetables
The Local Farm Stand.technology_integration: Use apps or digital tools for digital shopping simulations.

The Local Farm Stand.name: Farm Stand Price List
The Local Farm Stand.overview: Students create a price list for a simulated farm stand, using quantities and the present simple tense for items and prices.
The Local Farm Stand.general_objective: Develop writing and basic economic literacy skills.
specific_objectives: List farm stand items with prices
The Local Farm Stand.technology_integration: Use digital tools to create price lists.

The Local Farm Stand.name: Digital Shopping List
The Local Farm Stand.overview: Students develop a simple shopping list with pictures, using a digital tool or sketching the app on paper. They practice quantifiers and polite requests to complete the list.
The Local Farm Stand.general_objective: Develop digital literacy and practical language skills.
specific_objectives: Create a digital or illustrated shopping list
The Local Farm Stand.technology_integration: Use apps or digital tools to create shopping lists.

Helping Around the House.name: Chore Scheduling App
Helping Around the House.overview: Students create a digital schedule or use a paper scheduler for household chores. They describe daily routines using the present simple tense and frequency adverbs like 'always' and 'sometimes'.
Helping Around the House.general_objective: Develop responsibility and planning skills using present simple and frequency adverbs.
specific_objectives: Schedule household chores for the week
Use present simple and frequency adverbs
Design a clear and organized schedule
Share schedule with family or classmates
Helping Around the House.technology_integration: Use digital scheduling tools and apps.

Helping Around the House.name: Family Chore Poster
Helping Around the House.overview: Students design a chore poster showing who is responsible for different household tasks. They use the present simple tense and vocabulary for chores (e.g., 'wash,' 'clean,' and 'sweep').
Helping Around the House.general_objective: Develop visual design and writing skills related to household vocabulary.
specific_objectives: Assign household tasks to family members
Use present simple and chore vocabulary
Design an attractive and clear poster
Helping Around the House.technology_integration: Use digital design tools for the poster.

Helping Around the House.name: Chore Mural
Helping Around the House.overview: Students each create a picture for a large classroom mural that shows various chores being completed.
Helping Around the House.general_objective: Develop creative expression and vocabulary related to household chores.
specific_objectives: Draw and label a chore scene
Contribute to a collaborative classroom mural

Time for Exercise.name: Fitness Routine Guide
Time for Exercise.overview: Students write a fitness routine guide, using modal verbs like 'can' and 'cannot' to describe abilities and routines for sports or exercises.
Time for Exercise.general_objective: Develop writing skills and health awareness using modal verbs.
Time for Exercise.technology_integration: Use digital tools for virtual exercise tracking.

Time for Exercise.name: Exercise Plan Poster
Time for Exercise.overview: Students design posters showing different physical activities and sports, using present simple tense and modals like 'can' to describe what they can do to stay active.
Time for Exercise.general_objective: Develop visual communication and health vocabulary.
specific_objectives: Design a clear and colorful poster
Time for Exercise.technology_integration: Use digital tools for designing posters.

Time for Exercise.name: Virtual Exercise Challenge
Time for Exercise.overview: Students participate in a virtual exercise challenge by selecting an exercise challenge video or creating their own, recording their activities, and using the present simple and modals to describe their exercises.
Time for Exercise.general_objective: Develop digital literacy and oral communication through an exercise challenge.
specific_objectives: Select or create an exercise challenge video
Use present simple and modals in narration
Time for Exercise.technology_integration: Use digital tools for virtual exercise tracking and video creation.

Recycling for Our World.name: Recycling Station Poster
Recycling for Our World.overview: Students design posters explaining how to use a classroom recycling station, using the present simple tense and modal verbs like 'can' (e.g., 'You can recycle plastic bottles here.').
Recycling for Our World.general_objective: Develop environmental awareness and instructional writing skills.
specific_objectives: Design a clear and informative poster
Display poster near the recycling station
Recycling for Our World.technology_integration: Use digital tools for poster design.

Recycling for Our World.name: Recycling Data Chart
Recycling for Our World.overview: Students collect data on the classroom's recycling habits and create a chart to present the findings, using frequency adverbs like 'always' and 'every week'.
Recycling for Our World.general_objective: Develop data literacy and reporting skills.
materials: Graph paper or digital chart tool
Recycling for Our World.technology_integration: Use digital tools for chart creation.

Recycling for Our World.name: Recycling Campaign Video
Recycling for Our World.overview: Students create a video campaign encouraging recycling at school. They use the present simple tense and modal verbs to describe actions for sustainability.
Recycling for Our World.general_objective: Develop digital media and persuasive communication skills.
specific_objectives: Write and record a recycling campaign message
Use present simple and modal verbs
Recycling for Our World.technology_integration: Use video editing tools for campaign creation.

Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.name: Daily Conservation Log
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.overview: Students keep a daily log of their conservation activities, writing their actions in the imperative form (e.g., 'Turn off the lights to save energy.').
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.general_objective: Develop environmental habits and writing skills using imperatives.
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.technology_integration: Use digital tracking tools and apps.

Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.name: Conservation vs. Recycling Poster
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.overview: Students create a poster comparing different conservation actions and their benefits, using comparatives and superlatives (e.g., 'Saving water is easier than recycling.').
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.general_objective: Develop comparative language and environmental literacy.
specific_objectives: Design an organized and visual poster

Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.name: Energy Saving Challenge
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.overview: Students participate in an energy-saving challenge by tracking their energy conservation efforts over a week and using comparatives to describe results.
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.general_objective: Develop environmental responsibility and comparative language through action.
specific_objectives: Track energy-saving efforts for one week
Reflect on impact and share findings
Conservation Morning, Noon, and Night!.technology_integration: Use digital tracking tools and apps to log activities.

Planning Meals for My Family.name: Healthy Meal Plan
Planning Meals for My Family.overview: Students create a meal plan for their family, using countable and uncountable nouns with quantifiers (e.g., 'a few apples' or 'some rice') and modal verbs to describe preparation (e.g., 'We can cook beans.').
Planning Meals for My Family.general_objective: Develop health literacy and writing skills using food vocabulary.
specific_objectives: Plan healthy meals for the week
Share meal plan with family or class
Planning Meals for My Family.technology_integration: Use digital tools for meal plan creation.

Planning Meals for My Family.name: Digital Recipe Book
Planning Meals for My Family.overview: Students collaborate to create a digital recipe book featuring healthy meals. They describe the ingredients and steps using the present simple tense.
Planning Meals for My Family.general_objective: Develop collaborative writing and digital literacy skills.
specific_objectives: Write a simple recipe with ingredients and steps
Collaborate with peers on the book
Share the digital recipe book with class
Planning Meals for My Family.technology_integration: Use digital tools for recipe book creation.

Planning Meals for My Family.name: Nutrition Poster
Planning Meals for My Family.overview: Students design a nutrition poster highlighting the benefits of different foods, using quantifiers and the present simple tense (e.g., 'Fruits and vegetables are good for you.').
Planning Meals for My Family.general_objective: Develop health awareness and visual communication skills.
specific_objectives: Design a clear and colorful poster
Planning Meals for My Family.technology_integration: Use digital tools for poster design.
